advantages: Strong out-of-the-box performance on tabular data with minimal preprocessing—no feature scaling required for tree splits.
Bagging plus random feature subsets reduce variance of high-variance trees without a sequential training loop.
Trees train independently and parallelize well across CPU cores (n_jobs).
Robust to outliers and mixed feature types; handles non-linear interactions automatically through splits.
Out-of-bag scoring provides a built-in generalization estimate when bootstrap=True.
disadvantages: Often slightly less accurate than well-tuned gradient boosting on competitive tabular benchmarks.
Large n_estimators increases model size and inference latency—every prediction sums many trees.
Less interpretable than a single shallow tree; feature importance is heuristic, not causal.
Struggles with very high-cardinality sparse text or raw images without feature engineering compared with deep learning.
